memories.title: Classic move rule, not the prototype's lenient rule
memories.hook: Drop only onto empty tube or same-color top.
memories.body: The shipped game uses the classic ball-sort rule: a ball may move only onto an empty tube or onto a tube whose top ball is the same color (and not full). The ballsortgame.html prototype used a lenient any-ball-any-space rule — do NOT carry that over. Classic is the proven, strategic, monetizable formula and is what requires the solver/generator.

memories.title: Levels are infinite + generated, and must be guaranteed solvable
memories.hook: Generator + solver; every level provably winnable.
memories.body: No hand-authored level packs in v1. A generator produces endless levels on a rising difficulty curve. Because the classic rule makes random shuffles often impossible, generation must guarantee solvability. As of E5 this is done by random-fill + solver verification (see m11), NOT the original reverse-move scrambling — that produced trivial levels. The solver doubles as the hints engine.

memories.title: Stack: native SwiftUI, universal iPhone+iPad, portrait-only
memories.hook: SwiftUI, no cross-platform, portrait-locked universal app.
memories.body: Native SwiftUI (no Flutter/RN/Unity). Universal app targeting iPhone and iPad, locked to portrait orientation. Faithful port of the prototype's wooden-tray + glossy-ball aesthetic. Claude builds, Tom reviews.

memories.title: v1 is free, no ads, no SDKs
memories.body: v1 ships free with zero ad/analytics SDKs — cleanest build, fastest to store, trivial privacy nutrition label. Monetization (ads + remove-ads IAP, or paid, or skins-as-IAP) is deferred post-launch.

memories.title: 'Full TDD' on SwiftUI means MVVM + tested ViewModels, not view-equivalent web tests
memories.hook: No renderToStaticMarkup equivalent; weight is on logic core + ViewModels.
memories.body: Tom wants full TDD (carried from know-the-night). SwiftUI has no cheap markup-assertion equivalent. Honest plan: a pure, exhaustively-tested logic core (model/rules/solver) + MVVM ViewModels carry the testable weight; views are checked via snapshot tests or ViewInspector, which are weaker/flakier than web SSR assertions. View-test tooling is an early ADR (E0).

memories.title: Apple Developer enrollment is the long-pole external blocker — Tom owns it
memories.hook: Not enrolled yet; gates Game Center, TestFlight, submission. Start now.
memories.body: No Apple Developer account yet (~$99/yr, payment + Apple ID, approval can take a day+). It blocks Game Center, TestFlight, and submission. Per how we work, prod/account/payment actions are Tom's to run. Game Center (E10) and Release (E11) are intentionally late, isolated epics so the playable core is never blocked — but enrollment should start day one to overlap the build.

memories.title: Genre is saturated — pick a differentiator before store assets
memories.hook: A faithful clone risks invisibility; differentiation deferred but mandatory to revisit.
memories.body: Ball-sort/water-sort is a crowded genre. v1 scope is intentionally a clean classic build, but a 1:1 clone risks zero discoverability. Before spending on store screenshots/metadata (E11), decide on a hook: signature theme/skin identity, a twist mechanic, or a daily shared puzzle. Flagged, not yet scoped.

memories.title: v1 feature scope
memories.hook: Undo/restart/hints, sound+haptics, Game Center, stats+streaks, persistence.
memories.body: In v1: classic board with tap-lift/tap-drop + animation; infinite solvable levels; undo, restart, solver-powered hints; sound + haptics; Game Center leaderboards/achievements (gated on enrollment); stats + streaks; persistence (resume level + stats survive relaunch). Deferred: themes/skins, daily puzzle, monetization, any twist mechanic.

memories.title: Dev machine has only Command Line Tools — SwiftPM is broken, no Swift builds/tests until full Xcode
memories.hook: RESOLVED 2026-06-25 — full Xcode 26.5 installed; `swift test` + `xcodebuild test` both green.
memories.body: RESOLVED. Earlier on 2026-06-25 the Mac had only Command Line Tools (Swift 6.1.2), whose PackageDescription ManifestAPI was missing Package.__allocating_init, so SwiftPM couldn't link ANY manifest (even `swift package init` failed) — blocking all Swift build/test. Tom then installed full Xcode 26.5 (17F42); xcode-select now points at /Applications/Xcode.app. Verified working: `swift test` passes BallColor in BallSortCore, and `xcodegen generate` + `xcodebuild test -scheme BallSortApp` on the iPhone 17 Pro simulator passes the app SmokeTests. Keep for the lesson: a CLT-only Mac cannot build SwiftPM; full Xcode is required.

memories.title: E0 architecture is decided — see docs/TECHNICAL_DECISIONS.md; don't re-litigate
memories.hook: Layered MVVM (@Observable, iOS 17) over pure Core; Codable-to-disk persistence; snapshot+VM tests; XcodeGen.
memories.body: ADRs 0001–0004 lock: (1) Views→ViewModels(@Observable, Observation)→Core(BallSortCore, pure value types), DI via LevelGenerating/Solving/PersistenceStore protocols, min iOS 17. (2) Persistence = Codable JSON in Application Support behind a PersistenceStore protocol, + @AppStorage for settings; SwiftData rejected. (3) Testing = exhaustive Core+ViewModel unit tests primary, targeted swift-snapshot-testing for ball/tube/board primitives, PR screenshot rule; ViewInspector rejected. (4) Xcode app target defined as code via XcodeGen project.yml (Claude authors, Tom runs xcodegen), app consumes local ./BallSortCore package. Full rationale + rejected alternatives in docs/TECHNICAL_DECISIONS.md.

memories.title: Generator is random-fill + solver-verified — reverse-move scramble made trivial levels
memories.hook: Difficulty = solver min-moves; rejection sampling; ≤5 colors; async in the VM.
memories.body: E5 (PR #17) replaced the E3 reverse-move scramble generator. Measured: reverse scrambling produced trivial ~2-move levels at ANY depth (depth 10→2.0 moves, depth 160→1.9) because the random forward walk equilibrates near the solved attractor — the scrambleDepth lever was inert. New approach: random-fill the exact ball multiset, verify with the Solver, accept only a solvable non-won board meeting a minMoves floor (rejection sampling); else the hardest found in the attempt budget. Random solvable fills are genuinely hard (~14 moves 4c/2e … ~22 5c/1e). API is now generate(colors:capacity:emptyTubes:minMoves:using:); Generator takes a Solving; capped at ≤5 colors because solver verification is infeasible at 6 colors. The App-layer DifficultyCurve (BallSortApp/Game/DifficultyCurve.swift) maps level→params over a rising minMoves floor (+ more colors, fewer empty tubes). Because generation runs the solver (~0.1–0.5s), BoardViewModel generates OFF the main actor behind an isGenerating flag. Amends m2; supersedes E3.2's original note.

memories.title: Repo is public so GitHub Actions (macOS CI) is free
memories.hook: Made public 2026-06-26 after a private-repo Actions billing block halted CI.
memories.body: tomzohar/ball-sort-game was made public on 2026-06-26. Reason: as a private repo, CI on macOS runners (billed at 10× minutes) hit a GitHub Actions billing block ('job not started because recent account payments have failed or your spending limit needs to be increased') — jobs failed instantly with 0 steps. Public repos get free unlimited Actions including macOS, so going public unblocked CI at no cost. iOS/Xcode CI MUST use macOS runners (Linux can't build SwiftUI/xcodebuild). If the repo ever goes private again, expect the billing gate to return.
